blocks.body: Sign rules for multiplication and division: (+) x (+) = (+), (-) x (-) = (+), (+) x (-) = (-), (-) x (+) = (-). Same signs give a positive result; different signs give a negative result.
An even number of negative signs in a product gives a positive result. An odd number of negatives gives a negative. Example: (-2) x (-3) x (-4) = -24 (three negatives = odd = negative).
Division follows the same sign rules as multiplication. Dividing by a number is the same as multiplying by its reciprocal, so the sign rules are identical.
